refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is solution-neutral, specifying 'support teleoperation' and 'communication quality' without prescribing how to achieve it. Therefore, it is atomic and does not require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design communication system with redundant data paths to mitigate packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control to dynamically optimize data flow
Establish communication quality thresholds and enforce packet loss limits through system-level controls
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and feedback loops between monitoring and transmission adjustment modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring and adjustment functions, supporting modularity. However, tight coupling may occur if feedback mechanisms are not well encapsulated.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to allow adaptive protocol effectiveness
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with available communication infrastructure
Operational policies do not restrict dynamic adjustment of communication parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining modular boundaries for monitoring and adjustment functions to minimize coupling and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to send and receive communication status and performance data

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network for transmitting data packets

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion as other modules rely on NGHE for communication.
Low containment since vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle functions, not communication protocol adaptation; would cause high diffusion if implemented here.
Partial relevance as teleoperation depends on communication, but protocol adaptation is more foundational and better centralized.
Poor fit; safety services focus on alerts and safety logic, not communication protocol management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it centralizes network communication management, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion of protocol logic.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause some coupling.
If network link interface changes, it may impact multiple modules relying on NGHE Solution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; the selected DP fits well within the NGHE Solution module boundary.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause some coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion, centralizing the adaptive communication protocol effectively. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, indicating poor fit for this DP, supporting the chosen IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring packet loss remains ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which owns the network communication management and protocol adaptation, aligning well with the FR.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; the selected DP fits well within the NGHE Solution module boundary.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with strong justification and no recommended revisions. The DP directly addresses the FR with an adaptive communication protocol meeting the packet loss requirement. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution module) shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, supporting architectural soundness. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol can maintain packet loss ≤1% under agreed network conditions.
Telemetry data dependencies are manageable and do not compromise module independence.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may cause some coupling, but is contained within the NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data confirming packet loss remains ≤1% during teleoperation under various network conditions.
Verification reports validating the adaptive protocol's effectiveness and module containment.
